AFLP
A program designed to support and empower expectant and parenting youth.
SIDS
A program designed to educate and reduce the number of deaths from SID and SUID.
PHPP
Provides support for perinatal hepatitis B prevention and case management.
Home Visitation Program
Provides in-home parent-centered emotional support, service referrals, etc.
Community Resources
Planned Parenthood - Madera Health Center
500 East Almond Avenue, Suite #1
Madera, CA 93637
(559) 675-1133 or (877) 855-7526
BOOK
an appointment with Planned Parenthood
Madera County Department of Public Health assists local providers in meeting CPSP certification requirements and provides technical assistance after they are certified.
Online Resources
Madera County Hotline for Mandated Reporting
For more information on where to contact the following services:
Adult Protective Services, Assistance Program (CalWORKS, Cal-Fresh, Medi-Cal), Child Welfare Services, Law Enforcement, Resource Family Approval.
Postpartum Support International
The mission of Postpartum Support International is to promote awareness, prevention and treatment of mental health issues related to childbearing in every country worldwide. Every woman and family will have access to information, social support, and informed professional care to deal with mental health issues related to childbearing.
Preventive Health Care for Adults
Preventative Health Care is regular medical care and healthy behaviors to avoid illness and disease.
WIC Program
WIC is a supplemental nutrition program that helps pregnant women, new mothers, and young children eat well and stay healthy.
Office on Women's Health
Office on Women's Health provides national leadership and coordination to improve the health of women and girls through policy, education, and innovative programs.
Additional Resources
California Department of Health Services
DHCS provides Californians with access to affordable, integrated, high-quality health care.
March of Dimes
March of Dimes provides support research, lead programs, and provide education and advocacy so that every family can have the best possible start.
California Department of Public Health
Center for Health Statistics and Informatics (CHSI) is responsible for vital records (birth and fetal death records)
National Center for Education in Maternal and Child Health
The National Center for Education in Maternal and Child Health (NCEMCH) provides national leadership to the maternal and child health community in three key areas--systems-wide program development, education, and state-of-the-art knowledge--to improve the health and well-being of the nation's children and families.
National Women’s Health Resource Center
HealthyWomen is dedicated to educating women so they can make informed health decisions, advocate for themselves and prioritize their health and wellness.
The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
ACOG’s mission is to support their members to improve the lives of all people seeking obstetric and gynecologic care, their families, and communities.
